reasoning: The closing price on 2025-11-21 was 48.70, which is near a local peak and slightly above the linear trend projection for 11-30 (48.11). The statistical forecasting tool gives a 0.41 probability of being above 48.7 on 11-30 due to mean reversion and the price being extended above the short-term trend. However, Baker Hughes has strong fundamentals, solid Q3 2025 earnings showing great RPO in their IET segment, and very bullish analyst ratings. Because of the slight downward pressure predicted for the immediate term (7 days), I place the 11-30 probability around 0.43. As we look further ahead (1 month to 12-23), the upward drift of the trendline (slope ~+0.02/day) brings the expected value closer to the 48.7 threshold, leading to a 0.47 estimate. By 3-months out (02-21), we expect the long-term positive market drift and company fundamentals to push the expected price past the current 48.7 mark, making a positive return slightly > 0.5, settling at 0.51. This satisfies the rule that uncertainty increases with longer forecast horizons (moving closer to 0.5).
